# attestto-trust

> Independent public mirror of national digital signature trust roots and intermediates. Hash-pinned, version-controlled, git history is the audit trail.

Part of the [Attestto Open](https://attestto.org) ecosystem.

## Why this exists

Most national PKI repositories are partially broken — wrong content-types, half-deployed HTTPS, mixed-case URL quirks, missing branches, dead links. Every developer integrating a country's digital signature stack hits the same wall.

This repo mirrors the binary bytes published by official issuers: as-is, hash-pinned, version-controlled. The legal source of truth remains the issuing authority. We are not a Certificate Authority — we do not issue, reissue, sign, or vouch for any certificate.

**Always verify the SHA-256 against the official source when you can reach it.**

## Countries

| Country | Code | Status | Branches |
|---|---|---|---|
| Costa Rica | `countries/cr/` | ✅ live | Persona Física + Persona Jurídica |
| Mexico, El Salvador, Panama | planned | — | — |

To add a country, open a PR following the layout below.

## Repo layout

```
attestto-trust/
  scripts/
    extract-chain-from-pdf.mjs  — extract certs from signed PDFs
    refresh-manifest.mjs        — regenerate manifest.json with hashes
  countries/
    <iso2>/
      README.md          — country-specific notes + CA hierarchy diagram
      current/
        *.pem            — active certificates
        chain.pem        — all-in-one bundle (root + intermediates)
        manifest.json    — sha256, subject, issuer, validFrom/To, role for each cert
      archive/           — superseded certs, kept forever
      samples/           — signed docs used for extraction (when redistributable)
  .github/workflows/verify.yml  — CI: verify all cert hashes on every push
```

## Using the certificates

### Drop into your trust store

```bash
git clone https://github.com/Attestto-com/attestto-trust.git
cp attestto-trust/countries/cr/current/*.pem your-app/trust-store/cr/
```

### Verify against the bundle

```bash
openssl verify -CAfile attestto-trust/countries/cr/current/chain.pem your-signed-doc.pem
```

### Verify a cert's hash before using it

```bash
sha256sum attestto-trust/countries/cr/current/root-ca.pem
# compare against manifest.json sha256 field
```

### Inspect the manifest

```json
// countries/cr/current/manifest.json
[
  {
    "filename": "root-ca.pem",
    "sha256": "a1b2c3...",
    "subject": "CN=CA RAIZ NACIONAL - COSTA RICA v2, ...",
    "issuer": "CN=CA RAIZ NACIONAL - COSTA RICA v2, ...",
    "validFrom": "2015-07-09",
    "validTo": "2035-07-09",
    "role": "root"
  },
  ...
]
```

## Adding a country

1. Create `countries/<iso2>/`
2. Add `README.md` with the CA hierarchy, official source URLs, and known quirks
3. Place active certs in `current/` as `.pem` files
4. Run `node scripts/refresh-manifest.mjs countries/<iso2>/current/` to generate `manifest.json`
5. Place any superseded certs in `archive/`
6. Open a PR — CI will verify all hashes

Certs can often be extracted from officially-signed PDFs. `scripts/extract-chain-from-pdf.mjs` automates this.

## Audit trail

Every cert added, rotated, or retired is a git commit with a message describing what changed and why. The full git history is the audit trail. `git log --follow countries/cr/current/` shows the complete certificate lifecycle.

## Costa Rica specifics

Costa Rica's PKI has two branches:
- **Persona Física** — natural persons (cédula de identidad)
- **Persona Jurídica** — legal entities (cédula jurídica)

Both branches are covered under `countries/cr/current/`. See `countries/cr/README.md` for the full hierarchy diagram and known quirks with the official BCCR/MICITT repository.

## Related repos

| Repo | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `attestto-verify` | Web component that uses these trust roots for signature verification |
| `cr-vc-schemas` | Credential schemas for Costa Rica — Firma Digital is the signing mechanism |
| `attestto-desktop` | Desktop app — verifies documents against these trust roots |
